Hey guys and welcome to Themeplate 17. This is called the Psycho-Cybernetic, borrowing a little term from Maxwell. Marks on this one and what we're doing on this themeplate is really honoring the resistance and difficulty your prospect is likely to have around the investment decision. So instead of downplaying how hard it is to make a decision, we simply validate it and then call on our prospect to temporarily put on the mask or assume the identity of someone capable of making that decision. So this is going to hit heavily on the buyer identification and binary choice triggers that we spoke about earlier in this module. Instead of really shaming and resisting prospects who still have resistance at this stage, we totally integrate it and we invite them to transform into someone who can overcome it. So we're literally coaching a mini transformation on the spot. We're transforming them into someone capable of making that binary choice. So this is how it is going to play out. We will start off with that reminder of the urgency without pouring it on too thick once again. Future pace, the outcome or big transformation, who they will become on the other side of this journey, link it to the person they must become right now to achieve it. This is kind of the big tandem here. Sell against indecisiveness. Call on them to become decisive and offer that binary choice. This is that big literal call to action. You must decide. This isn't something you want to beat around the bush around. You want to say it as clearly as possible. Then you could close with the minimum viable commitment that you've already worked through on several of these steamplates and an optional PS for a little bit of empathy is to show your own struggle with indecisiveness and how you may have overcome it in the past and closing with a second call to action around the binary choice. The optional PS is something that could work really well from an empathy perspective. We tend to forget how much we struggle with buying decisions and we expect our prospects to somehow not feel any fear or resistance around making a choice. So this is just about us having empathy for our prospect, dimsing that, oh wow, he kind of struggles with that too, yet he or she hasn't allowed that to hinder them in the long term. So that's an element you may want to weave in. Here is how we have put it into action. So two hours, Mick Jagger, Michael Jordan, and The Matrix. Or it's just a click away. So this is the, um, the psychosybernetics asking them to just put on that mask for a few minutes of someone confident and capable enough to decide and having multiple call to actions of just reinforcing the fact that a binary choice exists. Again, binary choice is the reason why one on one selling with follow ups can close 20 to 50%. Whereas online marketing closes at two to 5% anywhere between that it's because in the first version it ends in a choice. You know, it doesn't end in a whimper and a pixelized timer counting down to zero and you guys never having that final conversation. So this email is meant to emulate the essence of that. Actually we can't do it fully at scale because you're not going to show up in the living rooms of everybody on your email list, but this is at least going to create that vibe and, and, um, initiate those triggers of what's happening in that one on one scenario so that we can do it at scale, do it with 50,000 people in the single afternoon.
